intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, chatty voice that demystifies health data rather than clinical jargon
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warm, approachable language; use 'you' and contractions; celebrate small wins with personality
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward clear, authoritative, clinical language; prioritize precision and formal structure over warmth

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar for experiment navigation paired with card-based layouts for individual health metrics will feel organized without being overwhelming
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers fixed navigation + grouped, scannable information blocks over full-width tabs or lists
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer either tabbed navigation or a denser list view; needs exploration of horizontal vs vertical organization preference

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer clean, unfussy data visualization that feels calm and unhurried over dense or colorful dashboards
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into hand-drawn or soft-edged aesthetics, whitespace, muted colors, analog-feeling interactions
facade.rejectImplies: Users want more visual richness, precision, and digital polish—go toward modern UI patterns, vibrant accents, crisp geometry

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated transitions when viewing health metrics rather than instant page changes, creating a sense of continuity and reduced cognitive load
facade.label: Smooth slide-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Build metric cards and data views with fluid entrance animations, use motion to guide attention between sections
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize instant rendering, minimize animation overhead, use snappy transitions or no transitions at all

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard might feel less clinical and more approachable with dark backgrounds and rounded corners rather than bright, sharp interfaces
facade.axisTargeted: Light vs dark + Rounded vs sharp
facade.label: Dark mode with soft edges
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward softer, darker aesthetics—likely prefers calm, non-aggressive visual environments for tracking intimate health data
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clarity through contrast and crisp, clean lines—possibly values precision and quick scanability over visual comfort

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, chatty voice that demystifies health data rather than clinical jargon
evidence.implication: Lean into warm, approachable language; use 'you' and contractions; celebrate small wins with personality

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights about patterns
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations that encourage reflection

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights about patterns
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations that encourage reflection
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards oscillate between wanting headline metrics (BMI, steps, sleep hours) and wanting the narrative context that makes those numbers meaningful. This probe tests whether the user leans toward data-forward (prominent numbers first) or narrative-forward (story first, numbers second).
facade.axisTargeted: Numerical prominence vs. contextual framing — do metrics lead or does interpretation lead?
facade.label: Big number, small story
facade.acceptImplies: User wants scannable, quantitative-first design where numbers are the primary entry point and story supports them. Design should feature large type, rankings, progress bars, minimal prose.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants interpretive-first design where context and insight come first, numbers are evidence. Design should lead with observations, findings, maybe hide raw numbers or integrate them into sentences.

facade.hypothesis: Users want a friendly, named character (e.g., a health guide) to personalize the dashboard and make progress feel like a shared journey rather than solo data tracking
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — branded/character-driven vs neutral/tool-like
facade.label: Branded mascot character
facade.acceptImplies: Introduce a consistent mascot or character that guides users through experiments, celebrates wins, and adds warmth and personality to the interface
facade.rejectImplies: Keep the dashboard neutral and faceless; let data and user actions speak without anthropomorphic guides or branded characters
